name: Actuator backend killed mid-tick: orchestrator continues ticking
aim: Kill HTTP backend; orchestrator must tick >=3 more times; recovery dispatch succeeds

threshold.rationale: Backend failure must not halt the control loop

result.chaos_injected: httptest.Server.Close() for actuator backend mid-operation

on_exceed: Backend failure halts orchestrator control loop → system runs open-loop → no scaling decisions made

answered_questions.what_chaos_was_injected: httptest.Server closed for the HTTP actuator backend mid-operation
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: >=3 ticks: control loop must be completely independent of actuator health
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Backend failure causes goroutine panic → control loop stops → no scaling decisions
answered_questions.how_chaos_was_injected: backendSrv.Close() after warmup; orchestrator continues dispatching to dead server
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: New backend server + router.AddRoute() + manual Dispatch(9999) succeeds
answered_questions.production_equivalent: Kubernetes pod killed for actuator sidecar / external scaling API goes down

name: 10 concurrent goroutines (Broadcast/Ingest/Prune/Dispatch/HTTP) + context cancel
aim: Concurrent cancel while all operations active: zero panics

threshold.rationale: No concurrent access pattern during shutdown must cause panic

result.chaos_injected: 10 goroutines concurrent with context cancel after 300ms

on_exceed: Race condition during shutdown causes panic → process crash → Kubernetes restart loop

answered_questions.what_chaos_was_injected: 10 goroutines running concurrent operations cancelled after 300ms
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Zero panics: any panic during shutdown is a bug — send-on-closed-channel, nil dereference etc
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Shutdown races cause panics → OOM or crash during graceful termination
answered_questions.how_chaos_was_injected: 10 goroutines each doing different ops; cancel after 300ms while all active
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: N/A — concurrent safety test
answered_questions.production_equivalent: Multiple dashboard clients + active control loop + SIGTERM simultaneously

name: Memory pressure soak: 5000 events/s for 1m0s
aim: heap_growth<=2.0x, orch_ticks>=150, panics==0 under 5000 events/s for 60s

threshold.rationale: Fixed-size ring buffers + Go runtime overhead. Measured post-shutdown post-GC.

result.chaos_injected: 5000 events/s sustained for 1m0s
error_messages: heap_before=1941KB heap_after=574KB growth=0.2961x ticks=201 min_ticks=150 panics=0

on_exceed: Memory leak in hot path → heap grows proportional to ingestion → OOM kill → restart loop

answered_questions.what_chaos_was_injected: 5000 events/s for 1m0s via Store.Ingest while Orchestrator ticks
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 2.0x: ring buffers are fixed-size; growth beyond 2x means entries retained beyond capacity
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Memory grows proportional to ingestion → OOM kill → CrashLoopBackOff in Kubernetes
answered_questions.how_chaos_was_injected: 1ms ticker × 5 events/tick = 5,000/s across 10 services; orchestrator ticking at 200ms simultaneously
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: N/A — steady-state soak test
answered_questions.production_equivalent: Production traffic spike with sustained high ingestion rate

name: Clock skew in telemetry timestamps: staleness detection and nil-safety
aim: Future (+1h), past (-10min), zero timestamps: no panics; stale entries rejected; normal entries accessible

threshold.rationale: Clock skew is inevitable in distributed systems — must be handled gracefully

result.chaos_injected: MetricPoints with +1h (future), -10min (past), zero timestamps

on_exceed: Clock skew from NTP resync or distributed system drift causes panic in telemetry pipeline

answered_questions.what_chaos_was_injected: MetricPoints with timestamps: now+1h (future), now-10min (beyond staleAge=30s), time.Time{} (zero)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Zero panics: Store.Ingest and Store.Window must handle any timestamp without crashing
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: NTP resync or node clock jump causes MetricPoint with bad timestamp → panic → store crash
answered_questions.how_chaos_was_injected: Direct Store.Ingest with crafted MetricPoint.Timestamp values
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: Normal service still accessible via AllWindows after skewed entries injected
answered_questions.production_equivalent: NTP resync causing clock jump, or replica pod with wrong system time

name: Full system graceful shutdown under active telemetry load
aim: SIGTERM: shutdown within 10000ms, goroutine_leak<=5, HTTP stops accepting

threshold.rationale: Kubernetes terminationGracePeriodSeconds=30s; must complete well before SIGKILL

result.chaos_injected: context.Cancel() on full running system (SIGTERM equivalent)

on_exceed: Kubernetes SIGKILL after grace period → actuator commands not drained → physical system state diverges from controller model

answered_questions.what_chaos_was_injected: context.Cancel() while Orchestrator.Run is ticking with active telemetry
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 10s shutdown: Kubernetes default terminationGracePeriodSeconds is 30s; must complete with margin
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Kubernetes force-kills process → actuator.drain() not called → pending directives lost
answered_questions.how_chaos_was_injected: ts.Shutdown() calls orchCancel() + httpSrv.Close() + act.Close()
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: Goroutine count delta; HTTP connection refused after shutdown
answered_questions.production_equivalent: kubectl delete pod / Kubernetes rolling update

name: Orchestrator restart under live telemetry: Store windows survive
aim: Kill+restart Orchestrator; Store retains 3 windows; new orch produces Bundles within 5 ticks

threshold.rationale: Restarted orchestrator must immediately use existing telemetry — not wait for cold warm-up

result.chaos_injected: orchCancel() while system active; new runtime.New() with same Store

on_exceed: Restarted orchestrator starts cold → over-aggressive scale-down for first N ticks → service capacity reduced unnecessarily during restart recovery period

answered_questions.what_chaos_was_injected: orchestrator context cancelled; new runtime.New() created with same telemetry.Store
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 5 ticks: Store.AllWindows() should return existing data immediately on first tick
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Controller restarts cold → N ticks of degraded decisions → temporary under-provisioning
answered_questions.how_chaos_was_injected: orchCancel() stops Run() loop; same store passed to new runtime.New()
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: Hub.GetLastPayload().Bundles non-empty within 5 ticks of new orch start
answered_questions.production_equivalent: Kubernetes pod OOM restart with persistent volume mount for telemetry
